members.name: First Baptist Church of Woodland Hills
members.tagline: First Baptist Church of Woodland Hills
members.description: In the aftermath of World War II, the Los Angeles Baptist City Mission Society saw a need to establish churches in the San Fernando Valley , where the expanding population growth was an open door to fulfilling the Great Commission. They bought property in the Valley, and after years of planning, praying and envisioning, the launch service of First Baptist Church of Woodland Hills was held on May 7, 1950. The first congregants were a small group of believers who had moved into the San Fernando Valley. Many were returning World War II veterans and were anxious to have their own homes. The property had been a walnut processing plant and some of the buildings were still there and served as the sanctuary and Sunday school rooms. The area grew rapidly and so did the church. It became evident that a permanent structure was needed and the sanctuary and parsonage were built. Several years later the fellowship and educational building was built. Even with the added facilities, as the Sunday school grew some of the classes were held outdoors. Pews in the sanctuary were added in the early sixties. As the needs of the church changed over the years, modifications were made to the facilities. The parsonage was converted into the pre-school building; the church office was moved from the educational building to its present location near the sanctuary. During the earthquake of 1994 major structural damage was done to both the educational and sanctuary buildings. Upgrading was done on both facilities. The congregation of believers are still responding to the Great Commission, and firmly believe their greatest days of blessing and growth are yet ahead.

members.name: Health and Healing DPC
members.tagline: Health and Healing Direct Primary Care: The Impossible Made Simple
members.description: I am a a board-certified family physician, and I've been practicing in this community for over 8 years. I have a variety of experience in both the HMO and fee-for-service models, and have always felt that the relationship between a patient and his/her primary care physician was degrading. We get less and less time with patients, and spend more time on documenting the visit than listening. Direct Primary Care is a model that removes third-party billing and puts the relationship back at the center. As a physician, I am only accountable to my patients, which is how it was always meant to be. I am so excited to be bringing this model to Woodland Hills. It has been gaining in popularity across the US in both its simplicity in practice for physicians and access for patients.

members.name: Heaven's Hands Home Care
members.description: Heaven's Hands Home Care was born from personal experience and a deep understanding of what families carry behind closed doors. Founded in their hometown of Connecticut by sisters Shantel and Gabrielle, the business began with a simple but powerful mission: to provide compassionate, dependable care that families can truly trust. More than a decade ago, Shantel and Gabrielle witnessed the impact their grandmothers dementia had on her life and on their family. They experienced firsthand the emotional strain, the physical demands, and the challenge of finding care that felt both reliable and compassionate. Through that journey, they came to understand just how deeply families need support they can trust and how meaningful quality care can be during life's most vulnerable moments. Years later, that purpose became even more personal. In 2024, Shantel made the decision to relocate to Southern California with her family and build upon Heaven's Hands success in Connecticut. With that vision, Heaven's Hands opened an office in Woodland Hills, in the heart of the San Fernando Valley, to serve families throughout the surrounding communities. In 2025, Shantel passed away suddenly, leaving behind her husband and infant son. Her loss was devastating and life-altering, a painful reminder of how fragile life can be, and how essential care, support, and presence are when families need them most. Today, Heaven's Hands exists in honor of that journey and the love that shaped it. It is a tribute to kindness, compassion, family, and the belief that no one should have to face life's hardest moments without support. We provide care that supports not only the individual, but the entire family bringing compassion, reliability, dignity, and peace of mind into every home we serve. We are a family business built from love, driven by purpose, and delivered with care.

members.name: Mercedes Cusick Therapy Services~Trauma Therapist specializing in EMDR
members.description: I am a Licensed Marriage Family Therapist, Certified in EMDR, focusing on trauma issues and mental health. I am incredibly passionate about trauma therapy as I have seen firsthand how transformative the approach can be for clients, especially those with complex trauma. I have experience incorporating ego state work, polyvagal theory, and attachment into my EMDR practice, which comprehensively conceptualizes overall functioning and help you achieve lasting relief. With guidance, I can lead you to a deeper understanding of yourself, allowing you to access your strengths, develop new and healthy perspectives, experience emotional healing, and enrich your relationships, and living life with enhanced purpose, joy, and value. After getting sober in 2007, I have dedicated my life to helping people impacted by trauma and addiction. I have over thirteen years of experience working in residential treatment, outpatient treatment, transitional living, sober livings, homeless shelters, and counseling centers. I have helped hundreds of clients, first starting at Tarzana Treatment Center, Malibu Life Recovery Center, Renaissance Malibu, Hope of the Valley Rescue Mission, San Fernando Valley Counseling Center, and recent years at The Villa Treatment Center. I bring vast experience as a clinical trauma therapist, EMDR clinician, and addiction treatment to my practice. I received my Bachelor’s from Loyola Marymount University, Addiction Counselor Certification from CAADE, and my Masters of Arts in Marriage and Family Therapy – Trauma Specialization from Pacific Oaks College in Pasadena. Also, I am a certified EMDR therapist through EMDRIA. EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) is a therapy that enables people to heal from the symptoms and emotional distress resulting from disturbing life experiences. It rewires the brain establishing new adaptive neural pathways which help people become unstuck and move forward in their lives.

members.description: Mother, Advocate of a adult son on the Autism Spectrum. A supporter of Parents, Caregiver, and Sibling of an individual with Intellectual Needs. The Host of (A) Moms Happy Hour Weekly Podcast. POCWASN Org. offer (Self-care) fitness, wellness, counseling, and specialty services to parents, caregivers and sibling of special needs individuals. The purpose of the organization is to offer rejuvenation and mind/body health conscious services to the underserved parents, caregivers and sibling community. Our program participants (target populations) live with the responsibility of round the clock care for individuals that are unable to care for themselves. This includes meal preparation, (sometimes feeding), cleaning, entertaining, the provision of medicine, physical and/or behavioral therapy as well as much other daily life-sustaining necessities for an individual living with a disability. All of these daily and sometimes hourly duties can wear on the parents, caregivers, and sibling both physically and mentally. Without a break, the caretakers can burnout. POCWASN works to lift the parents, caregivers, and sibling out of isolation, protect them from societal neglect, and ensure their access to quality self-care service. Parents, caregivers, and the sibling who feel nurtured and supported are able to better nurture their families.
